Title: Generate critical-result alert when a panic-value lab result is finalized
Preconditions: Lab order resulted; result value flagged as critical/panic per reference range; ordering provider configured as alert recipient
Test_Steps: 1. Resulent a lab observation with a value beyond the panic threshold | 2. Finalize the result | 3. Inspect the alert engine queue for the ordering provider | 4. Open the ordering provider in-app inbox
Test_Data: Test=Potassium; Value=7.2 mmol/L; PanicHigh=6.5; Provider=Dr Synthetic
Expected_Result: A critical-result alert is generated for the ordering provider with the result value, panic flag, patient context and Pending acknowledgement state

Title: Critical alert not raised for in-range result at exact upper reference boundary
Preconditions: Reference range configured with PanicHigh=6.5 mmol/L; result entered exactly at the non-critical boundary
Test_Steps: 1. Result a lab observation at the exact upper normal boundary | 2. Finalize the result | 3. Inspect the alert queue
Expected_Result: No critical alert is generated; value at the non-panic boundary is treated as non-critical per range definition

Title: Critical alert raised one unit beyond panic boundary
Preconditions: Reference range PanicHigh=6.5 mmol/L exclusive; smallest reportable increment 0.1
Test_Steps: 1. Result a value one increment beyond the panic boundary | 2. Finalize | 3. Inspect alert queue
Expected_Result: A critical-result alert is generated because the value exceeds the panic threshold

Title: Critical result alert routed to covering provider when ordering provider is off-shift
Preconditions: Ordering provider has an active coverage/on-call delegation; critical result finalized outside ordering provider working hours
Test_Steps: 1. Configure coverage from ordering provider to covering provider | 2. Finalize a critical result outside ordering provider hours | 3. Inspect recipient resolution | 4. Confirm delivery target
Expected_Result: Alert recipient resolves to the covering provider per the coverage decision table; ordering provider receives an informational copy

Title: Unacknowledged critical alert escalates after timeout to supervising clinician
Preconditions: Critical alert delivered to primary recipient; escalation timeout configured to 15 minutes; supervisor defined as tier-2 recipient
Test_Steps: 1. Deliver a critical alert to the primary recipient | 2. Do not acknowledge within the timeout | 3. Allow the escalation timer to elapse | 4. Inspect tier-2 recipient inbox and alert state
Expected_Result: Alert transitions Delivered -> Escalated; a new notification is delivered to the tier-2 supervisor while the original remains open and auditable

Title: Multi-tier escalation chain walks all tiers when each tier fails to acknowledge
Preconditions: Three-tier escalation chain configured Tier1->Tier2->Tier3 with 10m timeouts; no recipient acknowledges
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a critical alert at Tier1 | 2. Let Tier1 timeout elapse | 3. Let Tier2 timeout elapse | 4. Inspect final state and recipients
Test_Data: Chain=Dr A->Dr B->Medical Director; Timeout=10m each
Expected_Result: Alert escalates sequentially through all three tiers; each tier receives a notification; final unacknowledged state is flagged for manual intervention and fully audited

Title: Acknowledgement at an intermediate tier halts further escalation
Preconditions: Escalation chain active; alert has escalated to Tier2 and is awaiting acknowledgement
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm alert is at Tier2 awaiting ack | 2. Tier2 recipient acknowledges the alert | 3. Allow Tier2 timeout window to pass | 4. Inspect whether Tier3 was notified
Test_Data: Chain at Tier2; AckBy=Dr B; Tier3=Medical Director
Expected_Result: Alert transitions to Acknowledged; the escalation timer is cancelled and Tier3 is never notified

Title: Generate drug-drug interaction alert at order entry for a contraindicated pair
Preconditions: Patient has an active medication on the medication list; clinician ordering a second drug with a known severe interaction
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient medication ordering form | 2. Enter a drug that severely interacts with an active medication | 3. Attempt to sign the order | 4. Observe interaction alert
Expected_Result: A drug-drug interaction alert is presented at order entry naming both drugs, severity Major, and requiring override reason to proceed

Title: Override of interaction alert requires and records a documented reason
Preconditions: Interaction alert is displayed during order entry; override reason is mandatory by policy
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger an interaction alert | 2. Attempt to override without entering a reason | 3. Enter a valid override reason and proceed | 4. Inspect the audit log
Test_Data: Override reason blank then "Benefit outweighs risk, monitored"
Expected_Result: Override is blocked until a reason is supplied; once supplied the order proceeds and the override reason plus user and timestamp are written to the audit log

Title: Allergy alert fires when ordering a drug matching a documented allergen
Preconditions: Patient has a coded drug allergy on the allergy list; clinician orders a drug in that allergen class
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm documented allergy exists | 2. Order a drug matching the allergen class | 3. Attempt to sign | 4. Observe allergy alert
Expected_Result: An allergy alert is raised identifying the allergen, the ordered drug and reaction severity, and requires acknowledgement before signing

Title: No allergy alert for a drug outside the documented allergen cross-reactivity class
Preconditions: Patient allergy documented for a specific class with no configured cross-reactivity to the ordered drug
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm documented allergy class | 2. Order a drug with no cross-reactivity to that class | 3. Sign the order | 4. Inspect alerts
Expected_Result: No allergy alert is raised; order proceeds normally without false-positive alerting

Title: No-known-allergies status suppresses allergy alerting but is preserved
Preconditions: Patient allergy list explicitly marked No Known Allergies
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm No Known Allergies asserted | 2. Order any drug | 3. Inspect alerts and allergy assertion
Expected_Result: No allergy alert is raised; the NKA assertion remains intact and is not overwritten by the order action

Title: Appointment reminder respects per-patient preferred channel selection
Preconditions: Patient has reminder opt-in with preferred channel set; reminder window reached
Test_Steps: 1. Set patient preferred channel to SMS | 2. Reach the reminder window | 3. Run reminder dispatch | 4. Inspect delivery channel used
Expected_Result: Reminder is dispatched only via the preferred SMS channel; email is not used unless SMS fails

Title: Reminder dispatch deduplicated when scheduler job runs twice in the same window
Preconditions: A reminder for an appointment has already been dispatched; scheduler job is re-run within the same reminder window
Test_Steps: 1. Dispatch a reminder for an appointment | 2. Re-run the scheduler job within the same window | 3. Inspect the patient notification log
Test_Data: Appt reminder already Sent; Job re-run within window
Expected_Result: No duplicate reminder is sent; the engine recognizes the existing dispatch and skips re-sending

Title: Reminder lead-time boundary - appointment exactly at window edge
Preconditions: Reminder lead time configured to 24h; appointment scheduled exactly 24h ahead at job run
Test_Steps: 1. Schedule an appointment exactly 24h from job execution | 2. Run the reminder scheduler | 3. Inspect whether a reminder is queued
Expected_Result: A single reminder is queued at the inclusive 24h boundary per configured lead-time rule

Title: In-app notification badge count reflects unread alerts and clears on read
Preconditions: User has three unread in-app notifications
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm unread badge count of 3 | 2. Open and read one notification | 3. Re-inspect badge count | 4. Mark all read
Expected_Result: Badge decrements to 2 after reading one item and clears to 0 after Mark all read; read state persists across sessions

Title: Email notification renders without exposing PHI beyond minimum necessary
Preconditions: Email channel enabled; minimum-necessary content policy configured to exclude clinical detail
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a notification eligible for email delivery | 2. Inspect rendered email body | 3. Verify links route to authenticated portal
Expected_Result: Email contains only generic notice and a secure portal link; no diagnosis, result value or other PHI is exposed in the email body

Title: SMS notification truncation and link integrity at 160-character boundary
Preconditions: SMS channel enabled; message template plus secure link approaches 160-character single-segment limit
Test_Steps: 1. Generate an SMS notification with template near 160 chars | 2. Inspect message segmentation and link | 3. Open the link
Expected_Result: Message is delivered with an intact clickable secure link; if segmented, concatenation preserves the full URL without corruption

Title: Pairwise coverage of channel x alert-type x recipient-role delivery routing
Preconditions: Channels In-App/Email/SMS enabled; alert types Critical/Reminder/Interaction; roles Provider/Nurse/Patient configured
Test_Steps: 1. Execute pairwise combinations of channel, alert type and recipient role | 2. Trigger each combination | 3. Inspect delivery outcome per combination
Test_Data: Pairs: InApp+Critical+Provider; Email+Reminder+Patient; SMS+Interaction+Nurse; and complementary pairs
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination delivers via the correct channel to the correct role with the correct template; no invalid combination delivers

Title: Identical critical alert for same patient and result deduplicated within dedup window
Preconditions: Dedup window configured to 30 minutes; the same critical result event is emitted twice
Test_Steps: 1. Emit a critical-result alert event | 2. Re-emit an identical event within the dedup window | 3. Inspect the recipient inbox
Expected_Result: Only one alert is presented to the recipient; the duplicate is suppressed and counted in the dedup metrics

Title: Distinct results for same patient are not erroneously deduplicated
Preconditions: Dedup keyed on patient+test+resultId; two different finalized results arrive within the dedup window
Test_Steps: 1. Emit a critical alert for result A | 2. Emit a critical alert for a different result B within the window | 3. Inspect inbox
Expected_Result: Both distinct alerts are delivered; dedup does not collapse genuinely different clinical events

Title: Throttle caps non-critical notifications per recipient per hour
Preconditions: Throttle policy set to max 10 non-critical notifications per recipient per hour; 12 eligible events queued
Test_Steps: 1. Queue 12 non-critical notifications for one recipient in one hour | 2. Run dispatch | 3. Inspect delivered vs deferred counts
Expected_Result: Exactly 10 are delivered within the hour; the remaining 2 are deferred to the next window; none are dropped

Title: Critical alerts bypass throttle limits
Preconditions: Recipient has reached the non-critical throttle cap for the hour; a critical alert is generated
Test_Steps: 1. Saturate the recipient non-critical throttle cap | 2. Generate a critical alert for the recipient | 3. Inspect delivery
Expected_Result: The critical alert is delivered immediately and is not throttled or deferred regardless of the non-critical cap

Title: Acknowledgement records actor, timestamp and channel of acknowledgement
Preconditions: A critical alert is in Delivered state in the recipient inbox
Test_Steps: 1. Open the delivered critical alert | 2. Acknowledge it | 3. Inspect the alert acknowledgement metadata and audit log
Expected_Result: Alert transitions to Acknowledged; the acknowledgement records the acting user, UTC timestamp and channel and is immutable in the audit log

Title: Acknowledgement state synchronizes across channels for a single alert
Preconditions: Same alert delivered to a recipient via both in-app and email; recipient acknowledges via in-app
Test_Steps: 1. Deliver one alert across in-app and email | 2. Acknowledge via in-app | 3. Re-open the email link and inspect alert state
Expected_Result: The single underlying alert shows Acknowledged on all channels; the email link reflects the acknowledged state and does not prompt for re-acknowledgement

Title: Acknowledgement blocked for a user lacking clinical acknowledgement permission
Preconditions: A non-clinical user (e.g., registration clerk) can view a critical alert summary but lacks ack permission
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as a non-clinical user with view-only access | 2. Open the alert | 3. Attempt to acknowledge
Expected_Result: Acknowledgement control is disabled or rejected with an authorization error; the alert remains in Delivered state and the attempt is audited

Title: Failed SMS delivery retries with backoff up to the configured maximum
Preconditions: SMS gateway returns a transient failure; retry policy configured to 3 attempts with exponential backoff
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger an SMS notification | 2. Force a transient gateway failure | 3. Observe retry attempts and intervals | 4. Inspect final state
Expected_Result: Delivery transitions Pending -> Failed -> Retrying for up to 3 attempts with increasing intervals; success transitions to Delivered, persistent failure to Failed-Exhausted

Title: Permanent delivery failure triggers channel failover and recipient fallback
Preconditions: Primary channel returns a permanent failure (e.g., invalid number); failover policy to email configured
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a notification on the primary SMS channel | 2. Force a permanent failure | 3. Inspect failover behavior | 4. Confirm fallback delivery
Expected_Result: No further SMS retries occur for a permanent error; the notification fails over to email and the invalid number is flagged for data correction

Title: Retry exhaustion of a critical alert raises an undeliverable safety escalation
Preconditions: Critical alert delivery exhausts all retries on all channels for the primary recipient
Test_Steps: 1. Force all channels to fail for a critical alert | 2. Exhaust all retries | 3. Inspect escalation outcome
Expected_Result: An undeliverable critical alert is escalated to a monitored safety queue/supervisor for manual outreach; the failure is logged as a patient-safety event

Title: Patient opt-out of SMS reminders suppresses SMS but preserves in-app record
Preconditions: Patient has opted out of SMS reminders but remains opted in for portal/in-app
Test_Steps: 1. Set patient SMS reminder preference to Opted Out | 2. Reach the reminder window | 3. Run dispatch | 4. Inspect channels used
Expected_Result: No SMS reminder is sent; the reminder is still available in the patient portal/in-app record honoring the channel-level opt-out

Title: Global communication opt-out does not suppress legally required safety notifications
Preconditions: Patient has set a global marketing/communication opt-out; a safety-critical recall notice must be sent
Test_Steps: 1. Set patient global communication opt-out | 2. Trigger a safety-critical/legally-required notice | 3. Inspect delivery decision
Expected_Result: The safety-critical notice is still delivered per regulatory exception; routine/marketing communications remain suppressed; the override basis is documented

Title: Opt-out change is effective-dated and does not retroactively delete sent records
Preconditions: Patient toggles a reminder preference; prior reminders were already delivered and logged
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm prior delivered reminders exist in the log | 2. Toggle the preference to Opted Out | 3. Inspect historical records and effective date
Expected_Result: The opt-out applies only to future dispatch; historical delivered records are retained with their original effective state for audit

Title: Repeated low-severity interaction alerts are aggregated to reduce fatigue
Preconditions: Multiple low-severity interaction alerts fire for the same patient during one ordering session
Test_Steps: 1. Order several drugs each producing a minor interaction alert | 2. Observe alert presentation | 3. Inspect aggregation
Test_Data: Multiple Minor interactions in one session
Expected_Result: Low-severity alerts are aggregated into a single grouped advisory rather than presented as separate interruptive popups, reducing alert fatigue

Title: Snoozed advisory re-surfaces at next clinically relevant trigger not before
Preconditions: A non-critical advisory was snoozed by the clinician during a session
Test_Steps: 1. Snooze a non-critical advisory | 2. Continue the session without the triggering condition | 3. Re-introduce the triggering condition | 4. Inspect re-surfacing
Expected_Result: The advisory stays suppressed while the trigger is absent and re-surfaces only when the clinically relevant condition recurs; critical alerts are never snoozable

Title: Override rate analytics surface high-override low-value alerts for tuning
Preconditions: An alert rule has accumulated a high override rate over a reporting period
Test_Steps: 1. Generate repeated overrides of a low-value alert rule | 2. Open the alert governance/analytics view | 3. Inspect override metrics
Expected_Result: High-override low-value rules are surfaced in analytics with override rate and counts to support rule tuning and fatigue reduction

Title: Outbound notification represented as FHIR Communication resource
Preconditions: FHIR notification export enabled; a notification has been dispatched
Test_Steps: 1. Dispatch a notification | 2. Retrieve the corresponding FHIR Communication resource | 3. Validate required elements
Expected_Result: A valid FHIR Communication resource is produced with status, category, sender, recipient, payload and sent timestamp conforming to the FHIR specification

Title: Order-driven alert maps to FHIR CommunicationRequest with priority urgent
Preconditions: FHIR enabled; a critical-result event produces a request to communicate
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a critical-result communication request | 2. Retrieve the FHIR CommunicationRequest | 3. Validate priority and references
Expected_Result: CommunicationRequest is produced with priority=urgent, subject/recipient references, and reasonCode referencing the critical result, conforming to FHIR

Title: FHIR Subscription notifies subscriber when matching critical Observation is created
Preconditions: A FHIR Subscription is active with criteria matching critical Observations and a rest-hook channel
Test_Steps: 1. Create a Subscription with criteria for critical Observations | 2. Create a matching critical Observation | 3. Inspect the rest-hook callback
Expected_Result: The subscriber endpoint receives a notification for the matching Observation; non-matching observations do not trigger the hook

Title: HL7 v2 ORU with abnormal flag triggers downstream critical-result notification
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound interface active; ORU^R01 message carries an abnormal/critical OBX flag
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ORU^R01 with OBX-8 abnormal flag set to critical | 2. Process the message | 3. Inspect the resulting notification
Expected_Result: The abnormal flag is parsed and a critical-result notification is generated for the resulted patient and ordering provider

Title: Malformed HL7 ORU with missing OBX flag is rejected without silent alert loss
Preconditions: HL7 inbound interface active; ORU message arrives with a malformed/missing OBX abnormal flag segment
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ORU^R01 with a malformed OBX segment | 2. Process the message | 3. Inspect error handling and queue
Expected_Result: The message is rejected to an error/dead-letter queue with a parse error; no critical result is silently dropped and an operations alert is raised

Title: Notification REST API returns only notifications the authenticated user is authorized to see
Preconditions: Two users with different recipient scopes; notifications exist addressed to each
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as user A | 2. GET the notifications collection | 3. Attempt to GET a notification addressed only to user B by id
Expected_Result: User A receives only their authorized notifications; direct access to user B notification returns 403/404 and the attempt is audited

Title: Acknowledge endpoint is idempotent under duplicate requests
Preconditions: An alert is in Delivered state; the acknowledge endpoint is called twice with the same payload
Test_Steps: 1. Call the acknowledge endpoint for the alert | 2. Call it again with an identical request | 3. Inspect state and audit
Expected_Result: First call transitions to Acknowledged; the second returns the same acknowledged state without creating a duplicate ack or altering the original timestamp

Title: Notification audit rows are append-only and tamper-evident
Preconditions: Notification lifecycle events are persisted to an audit table; DB access available for verification
Test_Steps: 1. Generate a notification through deliver/ack lifecycle | 2. Inspect audit rows for each transition | 3. Attempt an in-place update of an audit row
Expected_Result: Each lifecycle transition writes a distinct immutable audit row with actor, timestamp and prior/new state; in-place modification is prevented or detectable

Title: Time-zone correctness of notification timestamps stored in UTC and rendered local
Preconditions: Server stores timestamps in UTC; user profile set to a non-UTC time zone
Test_Steps: 1. Generate a notification | 2. Inspect the stored timestamp in the database | 3. Inspect the rendered timestamp in the user UI
Expected_Result: Stored timestamp is UTC; UI renders the correct local time accounting for offset and DST without drift or duplication

Title: Critical alert dialog is keyboard operable and announced by screen readers
Preconditions: An interruptive critical alert dialog is displayed; assistive technology in use
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a critical alert dialog | 2. Navigate and acknowledge using keyboard only | 3. Verify screen-reader announcement and focus handling
Expected_Result: The dialog receives focus, exposes role=alertdialog, is fully keyboard operable, traps focus appropriately and is announced with its content per WCAG 2.1

Title: Notification status not conveyed by color alone
Preconditions: Notification list displays severity and read/unread state
Test_Steps: 1. Open the notification list with mixed severities and states | 2. Inspect severity and unread indicators | 3. Verify non-color cues
Expected_Result: Severity and unread status are conveyed by text/iconography in addition to color, meeting WCAG use-of-color requirements

Title: Non-urgent notifications deferred during patient quiet hours then released
Preconditions: Patient quiet-hours window configured (e.g., 22:00-07:00 local); a non-urgent notification is generated within quiet hours
Test_Steps: 1. Generate a non-urgent notification during quiet hours | 2. Inspect deferral | 3. Advance past quiet hours | 4. Inspect release
Expected_Result: The non-urgent notification is queued and held during quiet hours and released at the window end; urgent/critical notices are not deferred

Title: Concurrent acknowledgement by two recipients resolves to a single owner without double action
Preconditions: An alert is delivered to a shared role queue; two members open and acknowledge simultaneously
Test_Steps: 1. Deliver an alert to a shared role queue | 2. Have two members acknowledge at nearly the same instant | 3. Inspect final ownership and audit
Test_Data: Queue=ICU Nurses; Concurrent ack by Nurse1 and Nurse2
Expected_Result: Exactly one acknowledgement is recorded as the owner via optimistic locking; the second receives an already-acknowledged response; no duplicate escalation cancellation occurs

Title: Decision-table coverage of interaction severity vs override permission vs hard-stop
Preconditions: Interaction severities Major/Moderate/Minor configured with differing override rules; contraindicated pairs may hard-stop
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger each severity tier during ordering | 2. Attempt override per tier | 3. Inspect allowed actions per cell
Expected_Result: Each severity tier enforces its configured behavior - hard-stop blocks the order entirely, major requires documented reason, moderate allows override, minor is advisory only

Title: SMTP outage queues email notifications without data loss and resumes on recovery
Preconditions: Email channel enabled; SMTP relay made unavailable; notifications generated during the outage
Test_Steps: 1. Take the SMTP relay offline | 2. Generate email-eligible notifications | 3. Restore the relay | 4. Inspect delivery after recovery
Test_Data: SMTP=down then restored; Notices generated during outage
Expected_Result: Notifications are durably queued during the outage and delivered after recovery with no loss; queue depth and recovery are observable and audited
